title: Public Good Provision Game: Decision Flow
description: A flowchart illustrating the strategic decisions and outcomes for agents deciding whether to contribute to a public good, highlighting the free-rider problem and Nash Equilibrium.
elements: Public Good (Cost C, Total Value V)
relationships: Player evaluates private value v_i against cost share; if v_i >= C/n, contributing is a dominant strategy, but free-riding is preferred due to the incentive structure, leading to a Pareto-inefficient Nash Equilibrium where the good is not provided, despite the social optimum being provision if V > C.
